entries.value: Target mean time to resolve (MTTR): SEV1 <1 hour, SEV2 <4 hours, SEV3 <24 hours, SEV4 <72 hours. Track separately by severity. Trending upward indicates process degradation or increasing system complexity.

Target deployment frequency is daily or more for high-performing teams (DORA Elite). Weekly deployments indicate moderate maturity. Less than monthly signals delivery bottlenecks requiring process review.

Target change failure rate is <15%. DORA Elite benchmark is <5%. Rate above 25% indicates insufficient testing, review, or staging validation. Measure as (failed deployments / total deployments) over 30-day windows.
Target service availability: Tier 1 >99.9% (8.76h downtime/year), Tier 2 >99.5% (43.8h/year), Tier 3 >99.0% (87.6h/year). Track rolling 30-day and 90-day windows. Breaches require incident review.

Target incident recurrence rate is <10%. Recurring incidents above 15% indicate ineffective root cause analysis or incomplete remediation. Every SEV1/SEV2 requires a blameless postmortem within 48 hours.

Target runbook coverage is >80% of known incident types. Each Tier 1 service must have at minimum: startup/shutdown, health check, scaling, and common failure mode runbooks. Review and update runbooks quarterly.

SEV1: Full customer outage or data loss risk. SEV2: Significant degradation affecting >25% of users. SEV3: Minor degradation or single-customer impact. SEV4: Cosmetic issue or internal tooling problem.

All SEV1 and SEV2 incidents require blameless postmortems within 48 hours. Focus on systemic causes, not individual blame. Document timeline, root cause, impact, remediation items, and lessons learned.
Automatic rollback triggers: error rate >5% within 5 minutes of deploy, P99 latency >3x baseline, health check failures on >10% of pods. All production deploys must have a tested rollback path.

On-call rotation: 1 week per engineer, no more than 1 in 4 weeks. Primary and secondary on-call required. Handoff meeting at each rotation. Compensation for after-hours pages. Target <2 pages per on-call shift.
